sometime soon.explanation_en: at some point in the near future
sometime soon.example_sentence: I hope we can meet for coffee sometime soon.

propose.explanation_en: to suggest a plan or idea for people to consider
propose.example_sentence: She proposed a new plan to improve the office environment.
propose.original_sentence: Propose one of these ideas to someone and then watch his behavior.

monopoly.explanation_en: a situation where one company or group has complete control of something
monopoly.example_sentence: The company has a monopoly on the local electricity supply.
monopoly.original_sentence: The postal system, long a government monopoly, should be turned over to private enterprise.

turn over.explanation_en: to give control or responsibility to someone else
turn over.example_sentence: The owner decided to turn over the business to his daughter.
turn over.original_sentence: The postal system, long a government monopoly, should be turned over to private enterprise.

sound.explanation_en: sensible, reliable, or based on good judgment
sound.example_sentence: His advice was very sound and helped me make the right choice.
sound.original_sentence: Whether these ideas are sound or practical is not the point.

practical.explanation_en: likely to be effective or useful in a real situation
practical.example_sentence: It is a practical solution to a common problem.
practical.original_sentence: Whether these ideas are sound or practical is not the point.

significant.example_sentence: There has been a significant change in his attitude.
significant.original_sentence: What is significant is how a person handles propositions like these.

give a second thought.explanation_en: to think about something again or consider it seriously
give a second thought.example_sentence: He rejected the offer without giving it a second thought.
give a second thought.original_sentence: If he laughs at the idea and doesn't give it a second thought, and probably 95% will laugh at it, chances are he suffers from tradition paralysis.

chances are.example_sentence: Chances are he will be late again.
chances are.original_sentence: If he laughs at the idea and doesn't give it a second thought, and probably 95% will laugh at it, chances are he suffers from tradition paralysis.

paralysis.explanation_en: the inability to act or make progress
paralysis.example_sentence: The company suffered from decision paralysis during the crisis.
paralysis.original_sentence: If he laughs at the idea and doesn't give it a second thought, and probably 95% will laugh at it, chances are he suffers from tradition paralysis.

receptive.explanation_en: willing to listen to or accept new ideas
receptive.example_sentence: The manager was receptive to my suggestions for improvement.

repellent.explanation_en: something that pushes away or discourages something else
repellent.example_sentence: Fear of failure is a repellent to new experiences.

pretend.explanation_en: to behave as if something is true when it is not
pretend.example_sentence: He pretended to be asleep so he wouldn't have to talk.
pretend.original_sentence: I don't pretend to be the smartest guy in the business, but I think I am the best sponge in the insurance industry.

make it a point.explanation_en: to make a special effort to do something
make it a point.example_sentence: I make it a point to exercise every morning.
make it a point.original_sentence: I make it a point to soak up all the good ideas I can.

soak up.explanation_en: to absorb or learn something easily
soak up.example_sentence: She loves to travel and soak up different cultures.
soak up.original_sentence: I make it a point to soak up all the good ideas I can.

experimental.explanation_en: using new methods or ideas to see if they work
experimental.example_sentence: The chef is known for his experimental cooking style.

expose.explanation_en: to give someone the opportunity to experience new things
expose.example_sentence: It is important to expose children to different types of music.
expose.original_sentence: Expose yourself to new restaurants, new books, new theaters, new friends.

breadth.explanation_en: a wide range of knowledge or experience
breadth.example_sentence: Her breadth of knowledge in history is impressive.
breadth.original_sentence: This gives you breadth and prepares you for larger responsibilities.

progressive.explanation_en: favoring new ideas and modern methods
progressive.example_sentence: The company has a very progressive approach to employee benefits.

regressive.explanation_en: returning to an older, less advanced way of doing things
regressive.example_sentence: The new policy is seen as a regressive step for the environment.
